LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITIONERS' CLAIMS FOR ADMINISTRATIVE AND TRADITIONAL MANDAMUS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, LEAVING HIS PETITIONER TO VACATE PER THE CAA AS HIS SOLE CLAIM. ALTHOUGH PETITIONER WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDING, IT WAS PURSUED FOR HIS BENEFIT AND THE RELIEF HE SEEKS IS TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD. IT IS A "FUNDAMENTAL PRINCIPLE THAT A MEMBER OF AN EMPLOYEE BARGAINING UNIT IS BOUND BY THE TERMS OF A VALID COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENT" EVEN IF "HE IS NOT A PARTY TO IT AND MAY NOT EVEN BELONG TO THE UNION WHICH NEGOTIATED IT." (RELYEA V. VENTURA COUNTY FIRE PROTECTION DIST. (1992) 2 CAL.APP.4TH 875, 882 CITING SAN LORENZO EDU. ASSN. V. WILSON (1982) 32 CAL.3D 841, 846.) THE MOU CLEARLY PROVIDED THAT BINDING ARBITRATION WAS THE FINAL STEP IN REVIEWING AN EMPLOYEE'S DISCIPLINE GRIEVANCE. ZARZUETA ESTABLISHES THAT RELIEF SOUGHT BY PETITIONER IS NOT AVAILABLE UNDER CCP 1094.5 OR 1085 AND THAT HIS SOLE REMEDY IS A PETITION TO VACATE THE AWARD UNDER THE CAA. PETITIONER'S CONCERN THAT RESPONDENTS MAY ARGUE THAT PETITIONER MAY NOT AVAIL HIMSELF OF A PETITION TO VACATE PER THE CAA IS UNFOUNDED BECAUSE RESPONDENTS WILL BE JUDICIALLY ESTOPPED FROM MAKING THAT ARGUMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Arbitration Award Vacated
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Russell Robinson of Law Offices of Russell A. Robinson telephone number 415-255-0462 for Plaintiff Peter Quiambao.
Boris Reznikov of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4296 for Defendants City and County of San Francisco, and Municipal Transportation Agency.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tenative ruling.
PETITIONER PETER QUIAMBAO's Motion To Have Arbitration Award Vacated is denied. Mr. Quiambao has not shown that there are any grounds to vacate the arbitration award under CCP 1286.2(a). The award itself shows that the arbitrator did not refuse to consider the July 27, 2015 email or Mr. Quiambao's argument that the April 20, 2015 incident was an accident. The arbitrator concluded that whether an accident occurred was not material to the controversy because such a finding did not "preclude a separate finding that Grievant committed one or more rule violations that warrant[ed] discipline." Even if the arbitrator's reasoning was incorrect, an arbitration award cannot be vacated for legal or factual error. Mr. Quiambao's other arguments lack merit. He does not cite to a provision of the Memorandum of Understanding which prevents consideration of discipline outside of the preceding twelve months and, even if it was a legal error for the arbitrator to consider other discipline, the error is not a basis to vacate the award. The arguments that dismissal of Mr. Quiambao was arbitrary and capricious and that the award is unsupported by substantial evidence are not grounds for vacating the award. Mr. Quiambao's request for oral testimony is denied on the grounds that the request fails to explain why oral testimony is necessary. Order and a separtare judgment signed in opne Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Arbitration Award Vacated
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Continued To Apr-18-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RUSSELL A. ROBINSON of Law Office of Russell A. Robinson, Tel No. 415-255-0462 appearing for Petitioners;
Deputy City Attorney BORIS REZNIKOV of City and County of San Francisco Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3800 appearing for Respondents.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S demurrer to petitioners' claims for administrative and traditional mandamus is sustained without leave to amend, leaving his petitioner to vacate per the CAA as his sole claim. Although petitioner was not a party to the arbitration proceeding, it was pursued for his benefit and the relief he seeks is to vacate the arbitration award. It is a "fundamental principle that a member of an employee bargaining unit is bound by the terms of a valid collective bargaining agreement" even if "he is not a party to it and may not even belong to the union which negotiated it." (Relyea v. Ventura County Fire Protection Dist. (1992) 2 Cal.App.4th 875, 882 citing San Lorenzo Edu. Assn. v. Wilson (1982) 32 Cal.3d 841, 846.) The MOU clearly provided that binding arbitration was the final step in reviewing an employee's discipline grievance. Zarzueta establishes that relief sought by petitioner is not available under CCP 1094.5 or 1085 and that his sole remedy is a petition to vacate the award under the CAA. Petitioner's concern that respondents may argue that petitioner may not avail himself of a petition to vacate per the CAA is unfounded because respondents will be judicially estopped from making that argument.
